exercises.prompt: Treaty language: « Les États s'efforcent de promouvoir… » obliges them to…

options.text: nothing binding — only to try (the escape hatch at treaty scale)
achieve the result within two years
pay a fine if they fail

exercises.explanation: S'efforcer ≠ devoir: the jurist reads 'encouraged', not 'obliged'.

options.text: must be transposed into national law
applies only in Brussels
is a TV programme

exercises.explanation: Directives set goals; each state transposes them — hence half of French political quarrels.

exercises.template: L'{{b1}} du pays à l'Union a demandé dix ans.

exercises.explanation: Siéger — the institutional verb for sitting.

options.text: where applicable / if the case arises
the fallen case
in any case, always

exercises.explanation: Treaty hedging 101: it applies only if the situation occurs.

exercises.template: Le débat porte, comme toujours, sur la {{b1}} nationale.
exercises.explanation: La souveraineté — the heart of every European argument.

exercises.prompt: Match each Europe word to its English meaning.
Put the words in order: "Europe is the art of compromise."
Listen to the jurist. What distinction does she draw?
exercises.transcript: Quand le traité dit « devoir », l'État est lié ; quand il dit « s'efforcer », il est seulement encouragé.

options.text: « Devoir » binds the state; « s'efforcer » merely encourages it
Both verbs mean exactly the same thing
Treaties never use verbs

exercises.explanation: The binding-vs-hortatory distinction — a continent of difference in one verb.

exercises.prompt: Read this column on EU multilingualism and answer both questions.
exercises.passageFr: Vingt-quatre langues officielles, des centaines d'interprètes, et pourtant : dans les couloirs de Bruxelles, une seule langue circule vraiment. Le français, jadis langue des traités, défend sa place de langue de travail — les textes fondateurs furent rédigés dans la langue de Molière, rappellent ses défenseurs. Le multilinguisme reste inscrit dans les traités ; le cas échéant, on s'en souvient.
exercises.passageEn: Twenty-four official languages, hundreds of interpreters, and yet: in the corridors of Brussels, only one language really circulates. French, once the language of treaties, defends its place as a working language — the founding texts were drafted in the language of Molière, its defenders recall. Multilingualism remains written into the treaties; where applicable, it gets remembered.

questions.prompt: What is the column's ironic observation?

options.text: 24 official languages, but one dominates the corridors in practice
Brussels has banned interpretation
French was never used in the EU

questions.prompt: The final « le cas échéant, on s'en souvient » is…

options.text: irony: the principle is remembered only when convenient
a legal obligation to remember
praise for perfect multilingualism

exercises.prompt: How does the column refer to French?

options.text: « la langue de Molière » — the classic periphrasis
« la langue de Shakespeare »
« la langue de Goethe »

exercises.explanation: Periphrases by author: Molière = French, Shakespeare = English, Goethe = German.

exercises.prompt: Listen, then choose what it means.
exercises.transcript: La directive doit être transposée avant deux ans.

options.text: The directive must be transposed within two years.
Member states shall endeavour to promote multilingualism.
The European Parliament sits in Strasbourg.

exercises.prompt: What does « la construction européenne » mean?

options.text: European integration
a member state
a treaty

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word (from this unit's vocabulary).
exercises.explanation: « Le traité de Lisbonne date de 2007. » — a treaty.

exercises.prompt: Match each word to its English meaning.
Put the words in order: “The European Parliament sits in Strasbourg.”
Speaking practice — listen, then say the sentence aloud and record yourself:
exercises.targetFr: La directive doit être transposée avant deux ans.
exercises.targetEn: The directive must be transposed within two years.

exercises.prompt: Speaking practice — say this sentence aloud and record yourself:

exercises.targetEn: The European Parliament sits in Strasbourg.
